content.problemStatement:
Sarthak got an empty magic grid of size N×NN \times NN×N on his birthday. The grid can only be filled with positive integers such that the bitwise XOR of all numbers from any row, any column, or any of the two main diagonals must all be the same! Can you help Sarthak fill this grid using **only positive integers** no more than 10910^9109?


Input Format

The first line of each input contains TTT - the number of test cases. The test cases then follow.
The only line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the size of the grid.


Output Format
For each test case, output NNN lines, each containing NNN space-separated integers, such that the grid satisfies the condition given in the statement.


Each integer should be positive and ≤109\leq 10^9≤109.
constraints: All printed numbers must be positive and not exceed 10^9
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the size of the grid.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output N lines. Each line contains N space‑separated positive integers (≤ 10^9) such that the XOR of every row, every column and the two main diagonals are identical. Print a blank line after each test case is optional but not required.

sampleTestCases.explanation: All cells are 1. N is odd, so each row/column/diagonal XOR = 1, satisfying the requirement.
First test case N=1: single cell 1, XOR = 1. Second test case N=2: all cells 1, each row/column/diagonal has XOR 0 (even count), which is the same for all.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput: (1000 lines, each containing 1000 times "1" separated by spaces)

solution.approach: A constant grid works for any N. Fill every cell with the value 1. For an odd N the XOR of N copies of 1 is 1; for an even N it is 0. In both cases every row, every column and both main diagonals have the same XOR (either 1 or 0). The value 1 satisfies the positivity and upper‑bound constraints, so the construction is valid for all allowed N.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^2) – we have to output N^2 numbers per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the output buffer.
